from manim import *

class HashedQLearningConcept(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Define Data
        voice_text = "To solve the memory problem, we use a Hashed Q-table. Instead of reserving a unique slot for every possible grid, we pass the grid state through a hash function to map it into a smaller, fixed-size table. This allows us to handle larger designs, though it can lead to collisions where different patterns share the same table entry, resulting in sub-optimal 'almost-checkered' designs."
        goal_text_str = "Understand the trade-off between memory efficiency and optimization accuracy in hashed RL."
        formula_str = r"\text{Index} = \text{Hash}(\text{MatrixState}) \pmod L"

        # 1. Goal and Formula
        goal_display = Text(goal_text_str, font_size=20, color=GREEN).to_edge(UP)
        formula_display = MathTex(formula_str, font_size=32).next_to(goal_display, DOWN, buff=0.3)
        self.add(goal_display, formula_display)

        # 2. Helper to create 4x4 grids
        def create_4x4_grid(pattern_type=0):
            grid = VGroup()
            for i in range(4):
                for j in range(4):
                    if pattern_type == 0: # Checkered
                        fill = WHITE if (i + j) % 2 == 0 else BLACK
                    elif pattern_type == 1: # Random-ish
                        fill = WHITE if (i * j) % 3 == 0 else BLACK
                    else: # Inverse Checkered
                        fill = BLACK if (i + j) % 2 == 0 else WHITE
                    
                    sq = Square(side_length=0.2, fill_opacity=1, fill_color=fill, stroke_width=1, stroke_color=GRAY)
                    sq.move_to([i * 0.2, j * 0.2, 0])
                    grid.add(sq)
            return grid

        # 3. Create Left Side Grids
        grid1 = create_4x4_grid(0)
        grid2 = create_4x4_grid(1)
        grid3 = create_4x4_grid(2)
        grids = VGroup(grid1, grid2, grid3).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.6).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1.5)
        
        # 4. Create Hash Box
        hash_box = Rectangle(width=2.5, height=1.5, color=BLUE)
        hash_label = Text("Hash Function", font_size=24).move_to(hash_box.get_center())
        hash_group = VGroup(hash_box, hash_label).move_to(ORIGIN)

        # 5. Create Q-Table
        table_rows = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(
                Rectangle(width=2, height=0.4, stroke_width=2),
                Text(f"Index {i}", font_size=14).move_to(ORIGIN)
            ) for i in range(6)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1.5)
        table_label = Text("Q-Table", font_size=20).next_to(table_rows, UP, buff=0.2)

        # 6. Arrows
        arrow_in1 = Arrow(grid1.get_right(), hash_box.get_left(), buff=0.1)
        arrow_in2 = Arrow(grid2.get_right(), hash_box.get_left(), buff=0.1)
        arrow_in3 = Arrow(grid3.get_right(), hash_box.get_left(), buff=0.1)

        arrow_out1 = Arrow(hash_box.get_right(), table_rows[1].get_left(), buff=0.1, color=YELLOW)
        arrow_out2 = Arrow(hash_box.get_right(), table_rows[4].get_left(), buff=0.1, color=YELLOW)
        arrow_out3 = Arrow(hash_box.get_right(), table_rows[1].get_left(), buff=0.1, color=RED)

        # 7. Animation Sequence
        self.play(FadeIn(grids))
        self.play(Create(hash_group))
        self.play(Create(table_rows), Write(table_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # Grid 1 -> Index 1
        self.play(Create(arrow_in1))
        self.play(Create(arrow_out1))
        self.play(table_rows[1].animate.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=0.3))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Grid 2 -> Index 4
        self.play(Create(arrow_in2))
        self.play(Create(arrow_out2))
        self.play(table_rows[4].animate.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=0.3))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Grid 3 -> Index 1 (Collision)
        self.play(Create(arrow_in3))
        self.play(Create(arrow_out3))
        
        collision_highlight = Rectangle(width=2.1, height=0.5, color=RED, stroke_width=4).move_to(table_rows[1].get_center())
        collision_text = Text("COLLISION", color=RED, font_size=18, weight="BOLD").next_to(collision_highlight, RIGHT, buff=0.2)
        
        self.play(
            Create(collision_highlight),
            FadeIn(collision_text),
            table_rows[1].animate.set_fill(RED, opacity=0.5)
        )
        
        # Flashing red effect
        self.play(Flash(table_rows[1], color=RED, flash_radius=0.5))
        # Custom there_and_back using sin for scaling pulse
        self.play(collision_highlight.animate(rate_func=lambda t: np.sin(PI * t)).scale(1.1), run_time=0.4)
        
        # Voiceover Text Rendering
        voice_display = Text(voice_text, font_size=14, line_spacing=1.2).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(voice_display), run_time=6)
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class Concept04HashedQLearning(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create the Grid Patterns (Left)
        def create_mini_grid(color_indices):
            grid = VGroup(*[Square(side_length=0.2) for _ in range(16)]).arrange_in_grid(4, 4, buff=0.05)
            for i in color_indices:
                grid[i].set_fill(BLUE, opacity=0.8)
            return grid

        grid1 = create_mini_grid([0, 2, 5, 7, 8, 10, 13, 15]).shift(LEFT * 5 + UP * 2)
        grid2 = create_mini_grid([1, 3, 4, 6, 9, 11, 12, 14]).shift(LEFT * 5)
        grid3 = create_mini_grid([0, 1, 2, 3]).shift(LEFT * 5 + DOWN * 2)
        grids = VGroup(grid1, grid2, grid3)

        # 2. Create the Hash Function Box (Center)
        hash_rect = Rectangle(width=3, height=2, color=BLUE)
        hash_text = Text("Hash Function", font_size=24)
        hash_box = VGroup(hash_rect, hash_text).move_to(ORIGIN)

        # 3. Create the Q-Table (Right)
        table_rows = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(width=2, height=0.5) for _ in range(6)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0).shift(RIGHT * 5)
        table_label = Text("Q-Table", font_size=24).next_to(table_rows, UP)
        q_table = VGroup(table_rows, table_label)

        # 4. Create Arrows
        arrows_to_hash = VGroup(*[
            Arrow(g.get_right(), hash_box.get_left(), buff=0.1) for g in grids
        ])

        # From Hash Box to Table Rows
        arrow_out1 = Arrow(hash_box.get_right(), table_rows[1].get_left(), buff=0.1, color=WHITE)
        arrow_out2 = Arrow(hash_box.get_right(), table_rows[4].get_left(), buff=0.1, color=WHITE)

        # 5. Animations
        self.play(FadeIn(grids), FadeIn(hash_box), FadeIn(q_table))
        self.wait(1)

        # Draw Arrows to Hash
        self.play(Create(arrows_to_hash))
        self.wait(1)

        # Show Mapping 1: Grid 1 -> Row 1
        self.play(grid1.animate.set_color(YELLOW), Create(arrow_out1))
        self.play(table_rows[1].animate.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=0.5))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Show Mapping 2: Grid 2 -> Row 4
        self.play(grid2.animate.set_color(GREEN), Create(arrow_out2))
        self.play(table_rows[4].animate.set_fill(GREEN, opacity=0.5))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Show Collision: Grid 3 -> Row 1
        collision_arrow = Arrow(hash_box.get_right(), table_rows[1].get_left(), buff=0.1, color=RED)
        self.play(grid3.animate.set_color(RED))
        self.play(Create(collision_arrow))
        
        # Flash for collision
        flash_box = Rectangle(width=2, height=0.5, color=RED, stroke_width=6).move_to(table_rows[1])
        self.play(FadeIn(flash_box))
        self.play(flash_box.animate.set_opacity(0), run_time=0.3, rate_func=there_and_back)
        self.play(flash_box.animate.set_opacity(1), run_time=0.3, rate_func=there_and_back)
        self.play(FadeOut(flash_box))

        # 6. Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\text{Index} = \text{Hash}(\text{MatrixState}) \pmod L"
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        
        # Use a simple Rectangle as background instead of BackgroundRectangle to ensure compatibility
        formula_bg = Rectangle(
            width=formula.width + 0.4, 
            height=formula.height + 0.4, 
            fill_color=BLACK, 
            fill_opacity=0.8, 
            stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(formula)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(formula_bg), Write(formula))
        self.wait(3)

        # Final Clean up
        self.play(
            *[FadeOut(mobj) for mobj in self.mobjects]
        )
        self.wait(1)

from manim import *

class Concept04HashedQLearning(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Formula at the top
        formula = MathTex(r"\text{Index} = \text{Hash}(\text{MatrixState}) \pmod L", font_size=36)
        formula.to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        # 2. Define 4x4 Grid Patterns (Left side)
        def create_4x4_grid(pattern_type=0):
            grid = VGroup()
            for i in range(16):
                row, col = i // 4, i % 4
                if pattern_type == 0: # Checkered
                    color = WHITE if (row + col) % 2 == 0 else BLACK
                elif pattern_type == 1: # Vertical Stripes
                    color = WHITE if col % 2 == 0 else BLACK
                else: # Random-ish / Collision state
                    color = WHITE if (i * 3 + 1) % 5 < 2 else BLACK
                
                sq = Square(side_length=0.3, fill_opacity=1, fill_color=color, stroke_width=1, stroke_color=GRAY)
                grid.add(sq)
            return grid.arrange_in_grid(4, 4, buff=0)

        grid1 = create_4x4_grid(0)
        grid2 = create_4x4_grid(1)
        grid3 = create_4x4_grid(2)
        
        grids = VGroup(grid1, grid2, grid3).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.6).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1)
        grid_labels = VGroup(
            Text("State A", font_size=16).next_to(grid1, LEFT),
            Text("State B", font_size=16).next_to(grid2, LEFT),
            Text("State C", font_size=16).next_to(grid3, LEFT)
        )

        # 3. Hash Function Box (Center)
        hash_box = VGroup(
            Rectangle(height=1.5, width=2.5, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.2),
            Text("Hash Function", font_size=24)
        ).move_to(ORIGIN)

        # 4. Q-Table (Right side)
        q_table_slots = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(height=0.4, width=2.2, color=WHITE) for _ in range(6)
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1.2)
        q_table_label = Text("Compact Q-Table", font_size=24).next_to(q_table_slots, UP)
        
        # Animations: Initial setup
        self.play(
            FadeIn(grids), 
            FadeIn(grid_labels),
            Create(hash_box), 
            Create(q_table_slots), 
            Write(q_table_label)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Draw Arrows from Grids to Hash Function
        arrows_in = VGroup(*[
            Arrow(g.get_right(), hash_box.get_left(), buff=0.1, stroke_width=3)
            for g in grids
        ])
        self.play(Create(arrows_in))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 6. Draw Arrows from Hash Function to Q-Table with a Collision
        # Grid 1 maps to Slot 1
        # Grid 2 and 3 both map to Slot 4 (Collision)
        arrow_out1 = Arrow(hash_box.get_right(), q_table_slots[1].get_left(), buff=0.1)
        arrow_out2 = Arrow(hash_box.get_right(), q_table_slots[4].get_left(), buff=0.1)
        arrow_out3 = Arrow(hash_box.get_right(), q_table_slots[4].get_left(), buff=0.1, color=RED)

        self.play(Create(arrow_out1))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Create(arrow_out2))
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Highlight Collision
        self.play(Create(arrow_out3))
        collision_rect = q_table_slots[4].copy().set_color(RED).set_stroke(width=6)
        
        self.play(
            Flash(q_table_slots[4], color=RED, flash_radius=0.5),
            Create(collision_rect)
        )
        
        collision_text = Text("COLLISION!", color=RED, font_size=24).next_to(q_table_slots[4], RIGHT)
        self.play(Write(collision_text))
        
        # 7. Final Polish: "Almost-checkered" design hint
        confusion_hint = Text("Agent confused between\nState B and State C", 
                              font_size=18, color=YELLOW).next_to(collision_text, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(confusion_hint))

        self.wait(3)

        # Optional Clean up for voiceover timing
        self.play(
            FadeOut(collision_text),
            FadeOut(confusion_hint),
            collision_rect.animate.set_stroke(width=2)
        )
        self.wait(1)

```json
  "id": "concept-05-dqn-approximation",

While this should theoretically handle massive grids, the training is often slower and less stable for these specific geometric patterns compared to simple table-based methods.",
  "goal": "Visualize the transition from tabular RL to Deep RL for pixel optimization.",
  "remarks": "Show a 5x5 pixel grid. Animate it 'flattening' into a 1D vector of 25 values (0s and 1s). Show these values flowing into an input layer of a Neural Network (circles and lines). The output layer should have nodes representing 'Flip Pixel 1', 'Flip Pixel 2', etc. Highlight the output node with the highest value, then show that corresponding pixel in the original grid flipping.",
  "formula": "Q(s, a; \\theta) \\approx Q^*(s, a)",
  "image_ref": "img-8.jpeg"
}
```

generated_code:
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ConceptAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create a 5x5 pixel grid
        grid = VGroup()
        pixel_values = [np.random.choice([0, 1]) for _ in range(25)]
        for val in pixel_values:
            sq = Square(side_length=0.5, fill_opacity=float(val), color=WHITE)
            txt = MathTex(str(val), font_size=24).move_to(sq.get_center())
            grid.add(VGroup(sq, txt))
        grid.arrange_in_grid(rows=5, cols=5, buff=0.1).shift(LEFT * 4)

        grid_label = Text("5x5 Grid", font_size=24).next_to(grid, UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(grid), Write(grid_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 2. Flatten to vector
        vector = grid.copy()
        self.play(
            vector.animate.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.05).scale(0.4).shift(RIGHT * 5),
            FadeOut(grid_label),
            grid.animate.scale(0.7).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
        )
        
        vec_rect = SurroundingRectangle(vector, color=BLUE, buff=0.1)
        vec_label = Text("1D Vector (25 values)", font_size=20).next_to(vec_rect, UP)
        self.play(Create(vec_rect), Write(vec_label))

        # 3. Neural Network layers
        hidden_layer = VGroup(*[Circle(radius=0.12, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.5) for _ in range(8)])
        hidden_layer.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.2).shift(RIGHT * 1.5)
        
        output_layer = VGroup(*[Circle(radius=0.1, color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.5) for _ in range(25)])
        output_layer.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.05).scale(0.8).shift(RIGHT * 4.5)
        
        # Connections (Sampling some lines for visual clarity)
        conn_in_hidden = VGroup()
        for i in [0, 6, 12, 18, 24]:
            for h in hidden_layer:
                conn_in_hidden.add(Line(vector[i].get_right(), h.get_left(), stroke_width=0.5, stroke_opacity=0.2))
        
        conn_hidden_out = VGroup()
        for h in hidden_layer:
            for o in [0, 6, 12, 18, 24]:
                conn_hidden_out.add(Line(h.get_right(), output_layer[o].get_left(), stroke_width=0.5, stroke_opacity=0.2))

        nn_title = Text("Deep Q-Network", font_size=24).next_to(output_layer, UP).shift(LEFT * 1.5)
        self.play(
            FadeIn(hidden_layer),
            FadeIn(output_layer),
            Create(conn_in_hidden),
            Create(conn_hidden_out),
            Write(nn_title)
        )

        # 4. Highlight highest value output node
        target_idx = 12
        self.play(
            Indicate(output_layer[target_idx], scale_factor=2, color=YELLOW),
            output_layer[target_idx].animate.set_color(YELLOW)
        )
        
        action_label = Text(f"Flip Pixel {target_idx}", font_size=18, color=YELLOW).next_to(output_layer[target_idx], RIGHT)
        self.play(Write(action_label))
        
        # 5. Flip corresponding pixel in the original grid
        original_pixel = grid[target_idx]
        new_val = 1 - pixel_values[target_idx]
        
        # Create the replacement pixel
        flipped_sq = Square(side_length=0.5 * 0.7, fill_opacity=float(new_val), color=RED).move_to(original_pixel[0])
        flipped_txt = MathTex(str(new_val), font_size=24 * 0.7).move_to(original_pixel[1])
        flipped_pixel = VGroup(flipped_sq, flipped_txt)
        
        self.play(
            Indicate(original_pixel),
            Transform(original_pixel, flipped_pixel)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Formula
        formula = MathTex(r"Q(s, a; \theta) \approx Q^*(s, a)").to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(2)

```json
{
  "id": "concept-01-granular-softmax-classification",
  "voice": "In real-world recycling, trash isn't just binary\u2014it's complex. To handle this, the researchers used a Deep CNN to map images into twenty distinct categories. After the convolutional layers extract features, a Softmax function turns those raw scores into a probability distribution. Essentially, it treats the scores as exponents, then normalizes them so the sum of all categories equals one. This allows the model to pick the most likely material, like distinguishing a pizza box from a newspaper with high confidence.",
  "goal": "Understand how Softmax transforms neural network outputs into a 20-category probability distribution.",
  "remarks": "Create a 2D image of a plastic bottle (input) on the left. Draw a series of vertical rectangles representing CNN layers. Use arrows to show data flow through these layers into a column vector of 20 raw values (MathTex labeled 'x_i'). To the right, animate the Softmax formula appearing. Then, transform the 20 raw values into a horizontal BarChart where the heights represent probabilities. Use 'Indicate' on the highest bar and label it 'Plastic Bottle'.",
  "formula": "softmax(x_{i})=\\frac{e^{x_{i}}}{\\sum_{j=1}^{n}e^{x_{j}}}",
  "image_ref": "img-12.jpeg"
}
```

Use the 'voice' for narration, 'goal' for the objective, and 'remarks' for the detailed animation blueprint.

Also, use this formula as a reference:
softmax(x_{i})=\frac{e^{x_{i}}}{\sum_{j=1}^{n}e^{x_{j}}}
generated_code:
from manim import *

class GranularSoftmaxClassification(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Load and display the image of a plastic bottle on the left
        # In a real environment, 'img-12.jpeg' must be present.
        try:
            img = ImageMobject("img-12.jpeg").scale(1.2).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
        except:
            img = Rectangle(height=3, width=2.5, color=WHITE).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
            img.add(Text("Plastic Bottle", font_size=20).move_to(img))
        
        self.play(FadeIn(img))

        # 2. Draw CNN layers as vertical rectangles
        cnn_layers = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(height=2.5, width=0.4, fill_opacity=0.6, color=BLUE_B, stroke_color=BLUE_A)
            for _ in range(4)
        ]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.5).next_to(img, RIGHT, buff=0.8)

        # 3. Draw arrows to show data flow
        arrow_to_cnn = Arrow(img.get_right(), cnn_layers.get_left(), buff=0.1)
        internal_arrows = VGroup(*[
            Arrow(cnn_layers[i].get_right(), cnn_layers[i+1].get_left(), buff=0.1)
            for i in range(len(cnn_layers) - 1)
        ])
        
        self.play(Create(cnn_layers), Create(arrow_to_cnn), Create(internal_arrows))

        # 4. Display raw scores as a column vector (MathTex labeled 'x_i')
        x_i_vector = MathTex(
            r"x_i = \begin{bmatrix} x_1 \\ x_2 \\ \vdots \\ x_{20} \end{bmatrix}"
        ).scale(0.8).next_to(cnn_layers, RIGHT, buff=1)
        
        arrow_to_vec = Arrow(cnn_layers.get_right(), x_i_vector.get_left(), buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(arrow_to_vec), Write(x_i_vector))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Animate the Softmax formula appearing at the top
        softmax_formula = MathTex(
            r"softmax(x_{i})=\frac{e^{x_{i}}}{\sum_{j=1}^{n}e^{x_{j}}}"
        ).scale(0.85).to_edge(UP, buff=0.6)
        self.play(Write(softmax_formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Transform the raw values into a BarChart
        # We use Axes and Rectangles to manually build the bar chart for maximum compatibility
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 20, 1],
            y_range=[0, 1, 0.5],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=3,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False, "stroke_width": 2}
        ).scale(0.8).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5).shift(DOWN * 0.5)

        # Mock probability distribution (20 categories, category 16 is the "Plastic Bottle")
        probabilities = [0.03] * 20
        probabilities[15] = 0.82
        
        bars = VGroup()
        for i, prob in enumerate(probabilities):
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=axes.x_axis.get_unit_size() * 0.7,
                height=prob * axes.y_axis.get_unit_size(),
                fill_opacity=0.8,
                fill_color=BLUE_C,
                stroke_width=1
            )
            bar.move_to(axes.c2p(i + 0.5, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN)
            bars.add(bar)

        # Transition: x_i vector transforms/fades into the probability bars
        self.play(
            FadeOut(x_i_vector),
            FadeOut(arrow_to_vec),
            Create(axes),
            Create(bars),
            run_time=2
        )

        # 7. Indicate the highest bar and label it 'Plastic Bottle'
        highest_bar = bars[15]
        self.play(Indicate(highest_bar, color=YELLOW, scale_factor=1.2))
        
        bottle_label = Text("Plastic Bottle", font_size=24, color=YELLOW).next_to(highest_bar, UP, buff=0.2)
        self.play(Write(bottle_label))

        self.wait(3)

from manim import *

class GranularSoftmaxClassification(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Input Image on the left
        try:
            # Attempt to load the referenced image
            input_image = ImageMobject("img-12.jpeg").scale(1.2).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
        except:
            # Fallback if image file is not found
            input_image = RoundedRectangle(height=2, width=1.5, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.3).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
            input_image.add(Text("Plastic Bottle", font_size=16).move_to(input_image))
            
        img_label = Text("Input Image", font_size=20).next_to(input_image, DOWN)

        # 2. CNN Layers (Represented as vertical rectangles)
        cnn_layers = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(width=0.4, height=2.0 - i*0.4, fill_opacity=0.5, color=WHITE)
            for i in range(3)
        ]).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.2).next_to(input_image, RIGHT, buff=0.8)

        # 3. Data flow arrows
        arrow1 = Arrow(input_image.get_right(), cnn_layers.get_left(), buff=0.1)

        # 4. Raw scores (MathTex labeled 'x_i' as a column vector)
        raw_scores = MathTex(r"x_i = \begin{bmatrix} 2.3 \\ 0.5 \\ \vdots \\ -1.2 \end{bmatrix}", font_size=36)
        raw_scores.next_to(cnn_layers, RIGHT, buff=0.8)
        arrow2 = Arrow(cnn_layers.get_right(), raw_scores.get_left(), buff=0.1)

        # 5. Softmax formula appearing at the top
        softmax_formula = MathTex(
            r"softmax(x_{i}) = \frac{e^{x_{i}}}{\sum_{j=1}^{n} e^{x_{j}}}",
            font_size=34
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)

        # 6. Horizontal Bar Chart (Probabilities for 20 categories)
        # Visualizing a subset for clarity while maintaining the '20 categories' concept
        probs = [0.03, 0.02, 0.05, 0.75, 0.02, 0.01, 0.02, 0.03, 0.01, 0.04, 0.02]
        bars = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(height=0.2, width=p*5, fill_opacity=0.8, color=BLUE)
            for p in probs
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.1, aligned_edge=LEFT)
        
        bars.to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1.2).shift(DOWN*0.5)
        
        # Label the highest bar (index 3 corresponds to 0.75 probability)
        highest_bar = bars[3]
        highest_bar.set_color(YELLOW)
        bar_label = Text("Plastic Bottle", font_size=24, color=YELLOW).next_to(highest_bar, RIGHT, buff=0.3)

        # --- Animation Sequence ---
        # Show input and CNN layers
        self.play(FadeIn(input_image), Write(img_label))
        self.play(Create(arrow1), Create(cnn_layers))
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Show data flowing into raw vector
        self.play(Create(arrow2), Write(raw_scores))
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Show Softmax formula
        self.play(Write(softmax_formula))
        self.wait(1.5)
        
        # Transform/Transition into probability bar chart
        self.play(
            FadeOut(raw_scores),
            FadeOut(arrow2),
            Create(bars),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Indicate the most likely material as specified in remarks
        self.play(Indicate(highest_bar, scale_factor=1.1, color=YELLOW))
        self.play(Write(bar_label))
        self.wait(3)

They manually segmented single objects\u2014like a can or a piece of cardboard\u2014and then used a script to randomly sample, scale, and rotate them onto a blank canvas. By overlapping these images at different angles, they forced the model to learn how to identify objects even when they are partially hidden or tilted, mimicking the messy reality of a recycling bin.",
```json
  "goal": "Visualize the process of creating a synthetic multi-object dataset using rotation, scaling, and layering.",
  "remarks": "Start with a blank white Rectangle representing the canvas. Display five segmented trash icons (e.g., a bottle, a can, a paper scrap) floating around it. Use a ValueTracker to show random numbers for 'scale' and 'rotation'. One by one, animate each icon moving onto the canvas, applying 'Rotate' and 'Scale' transforms as they land. Ensure some icons 'overlap' by placing them on different Z-indices, creating a crowded scene of 9 objects.",
  "formula": null,
  "image_ref": "img-0.jpeg"
}
```

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class SyntheticPileGeneration(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create a blank canvas representing the workspace
        canvas = Rectangle(width=8, height=6, color=WHITE, fill_opacity=0.1)
        canvas_label = Text("Canvas", font_size=24).next_to(canvas, UP)
        
        # 2. ValueTrackers for random scale and rotation values
        scale_tracker = ValueTracker(1.0)
        rot_tracker = ValueTracker(0)

        # 3. Parameter display labels
        scale_text = Text("Scale: ", font_size=24)
        scale_num = DecimalNumber(1.0).scale(0.8)
        rot_text = Text("Rotation: ", font_size=24)
        rot_num = DecimalNumber(0, unit="^\\circ").scale(0.8)

        # Group labels and numbers
        tracker_display = VGroup(
            VGroup(scale_text, scale_num).arrange(RIGHT),
            VGroup(rot_text, rot_num).arrange(RIGHT)
        ).arrange(DOWN, aligned_edge=LEFT).to_corner(UL)

        # Updaters for the trackers
        scale_num.add_updater(lambda m: m.set_value(scale_tracker.get_value()))
        rot_num.add_updater(lambda m: m.set_value(rot_tracker.get_value()))

        # 4. Define segmented trash icons (varied shapes/colors)

        ]

            target_pos = pos_data[i]

            )

from manim import *

class YOLOv10LocalizationLoss(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Background / Image Representation
        # Defining basic colors to avoid undefined identifier issues
        MY_BLUE = BLUE
        MY_YELLOW = YELLOW
        MY_RED = RED
        MY_GREEN = GREEN
        
        image_w, image_h = 6, 6
        image_bg = Rectangle(width=image_w, height=image_h, color=MY_BLUE, fill_opacity=0.1)
        image_label = Text("Input Image", font_size=24, color=GRAY).to_edge(UP, buff=0.2)
        
        # 2. 7x7 Grid (NumberPlane)
        grid = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[0, 7, 1],
            y_range=[0, 7, 1],
            x_length=image_w,
            y_length=image_h,
            background_line_style={"stroke_color": WHITE, "stroke_width": 1, "stroke_opacity": 0.3},
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False}
        ).move_to(ORIGIN)
        
        self.add(image_bg, image_label, grid)
        
        # 3. Highlight specific cell (where object center lies)
        # Using cell (4, 3) in grid coordinates
        cell_size = image_w / 7
        cell_center = grid.c2p(4.5, 3.5)
        highlight_cell = Rectangle(
            width=cell_size, height=cell_size,
            stroke_color=MY_YELLOW, stroke_width=4, fill_color=MY_YELLOW, fill_opacity=0.2
        ).move_to(cell_center)
        
        # 4. Bounding Boxes
        # Ground Truth: Dashed Yellow
        truth_box = Rectangle(width=1.2, height=1.8, color=MY_YELLOW)
        truth_box.set_stroke(width=4).set_stroke(dash_pattern=[0.1, 0.1])
        truth_box.move_to(cell_center)
        
        # Prediction: Solid Red, slightly offset
        pred_box = Rectangle(width=1.6, height=2.2, color=MY_RED, stroke_width=4)
        pred_box.move_to(cell_center + RIGHT * 0.4 + UP * 0.3)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(highlight_cell))
        self.play(Create(truth_box), Create(pred_box))
        
        # 5. Arrows for Delta X, Delta Y
        gt_center = truth_box.get_center()
        pred_center = pred_box.get_center()
        
        # We'll use simple Arrow objects as they are highly compatible
        arrow_x = Arrow(
            start=[gt_center[0], pred_center[1], 0],
            end=pred_center,
            color=MY_BLUE, buff=0, tip_length=0.15
        )
        arrow_y = Arrow(
            start=[gt_center[0], pred_center[1], 0],
            end=gt_center,
            color=MY_GREEN, buff=0, tip_length=0.15
        )
        
        label_x = MathTex(r"x - \hat{x}", font_size=20, color=MY_BLUE).next_to(arrow_x, UP, buff=0.05)
        label_y = MathTex(r"y - \hat{y}", font_size=20, color=MY_GREEN).next_to(arrow_y, LEFT, buff=0.05)

        # 6. Localization Loss Formula
        # Splitting into two parts for highlighting logic
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\lambda_{coord}\sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}\left((x_{i}-\hat{x}_{i})^{2}+(y_{i}-\hat{y}_{i})^{2}\right)",
            r" + \sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}\left((\sqrt{w_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{w}_{i}})^{2}+(\sqrt{h_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{h}_{i}})^{2}\right)",
            font_size=24
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)

        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(Create(arrow_x), Write(label_x), Create(arrow_y), Write(label_y))
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Mock Training Animation
        # Align boxes and highlight formula terms
        self.play(
            pred_box.animate.move_to(gt_center).set_width(truth_box.width).set_height(truth_box.height),
            FadeOut(arrow_x), FadeOut(label_x),
            FadeOut(arrow_y), FadeOut(label_y),
            formula[0].animate.set_color(MY_YELLOW),
            run_time=2
        )
        
        self.play(
            formula[0].animate.set_color(WHITE),
            formula[1].animate.set_color(MY_YELLOW),
            run_time=1
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class ConceptYolov10LocalizationLoss(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Background and 7x7 Grid
        # Using a background rectangle since external images might not be available
        image_bg = Rectangle(width=14, height=8, fill_color=DARK_GRAY, fill_opacity=0.4)
        image_text = Text("Image: Multiple Trash Items", font_size=24, color=WHITE, opacity=0.5).to_edge(UP)
        
        # 7x7 Grid (NumberPlane)
        # 7 units wide/high, centered at -3 on the x-axis to leave room for formula
        grid = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-3.5, 3.5, 1],
            y_range=[-3.5, 3.5, 1],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=6,
            background_line_style={"stroke_color": BLUE, "stroke_width": 2, "stroke_opacity": 0.5},
            axis_config={"include_numbers": False}
        ).shift(LEFT * 3)
        
        self.add(image_bg, image_text, grid)

        # 2. Highlight a specific cell (soda can center)
        # Let's pick cell (1, 1) in coordinate space
        cell_highlight = Rectangle(width=6/7, height=6/7, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=4)
        cell_highlight.move_to(grid.c2p(1, 1))
        
        obj_center = Dot(grid.c2p(1.2, 0.8), color=RED, radius=0.08)
        obj_label = Text("Object Center", font_size=14, color=RED).next_to(obj_center, UR, buff=0.05)

        self.play(Create(cell_highlight), FadeIn(obj_center), Write(obj_label))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Ground Truth Box (Dashed) and Predicted Box (Solid)
        # GT Box
        gt_w, gt_h = 1.5, 2.0
        gt_rect_base = Rectangle(width=gt_w, height=gt_h, color=WHITE).move_to(grid.c2p(1.2, 0.8))
        # Create dashed effect using DashedLine segments to avoid "DashedVMobject" errors
        gt_corners = [gt_rect_base.get_corner(UL), gt_rect_base.get_corner(UR), gt_rect_base.get_corner(DR), gt_rect_base.get_corner(DL)]
        gt_dashed = VGroup(*[
            DashedLine(gt_corners[i], gt_corners[(i+1)%4], color=WHITE) for i in range(4)
        ])
        gt_label = Text("Ground Truth", font_size=14, color=WHITE).next_to(gt_dashed, UP, buff=0.1)

        # Predicted Box
        pred_w, pred_h = 2.0, 2.6
        pred_box = Rectangle(width=pred_w, height=pred_h, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=3).move_to(grid.c2p(1.7, 0.4))
        pred_label = Text("Prediction", font_size=14, color=YELLOW).next_to(pred_box, DOWN, buff=0.1)

        self.play(Create(gt_dashed), Write(gt_label))
        self.play(Create(pred_box), Write(pred_label))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 4. Arrows for x, y distance and w, h difference
        arrow_x = Arrow(start=pred_box.get_center(), end=[gt_rect_base.get_x(), pred_box.get_y(), 0], buff=0, color=BLUE, stroke_width=3)
        arrow_y = Arrow(start=[gt_rect_base.get_x(), pred_box.get_y(), 0], end=gt_rect_base.get_center(), buff=0, color=GREEN, stroke_width=3)
        
        # Dimension difference indicators (width/height)
        arrow_w = DoubleArrow(
            start=[gt_rect_base.get_right()[0], gt_rect_base.get_y(), 0],
            end=[pred_box.get_right()[0], gt_rect_base.get_y(), 0],
            color=PINK, buff=0, tip_length=0.1
        )
        
        self.play(GrowArrow(arrow_x), GrowArrow(arrow_y))
        self.play(Create(arrow_w))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. LaTeX Formula
        # Breaking it into parts for highlighting
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\lambda_{coord}\sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}", 
            r"\left[(x_{i}-\hat{x}_{i})^{2}+(y_{i}-\hat{y}_{i})^{2}\right]",
            r"+",
            r"\sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}1_{ij}^{obj}",
            r"\left[(\sqrt{w_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{w}_{i}})^{2}+(\sqrt{h_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{h}_{i}})^{2}\right]",
            font_size=20
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.3).shift(UP * 0.5)
        
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Training Animation: Align boxes and highlight formula
        # Highlight (x, y) terms
        coord_h = SurroundingRectangle(formula[1], color=BLUE, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(coord_h))
        self.play(
            pred_box.animate.move_to(gt_rect_base.get_center()),
            FadeOut(arrow_x), FadeOut(arrow_y),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(0.2)

        # Highlight (w, h) terms
        dim_h = SurroundingRectangle(formula[4], color=PINK, buff=0.1)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(coord_h, dim_h))
        self.play(
            pred_box.animate.stretch_to_fit_width(gt_w).stretch_to_fit_height(gt_h),
            FadeOut(arrow_w),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        
        self.play(FadeOut(dim_h))
        self.play(pred_box.animate.set_color(GREEN))
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class YOLOv10LocalizationLoss(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Image and Grid Background
        try:
            image = ImageMobject("img-11.jpeg")
            image.height = 6
        except:
            image = Rectangle(width=8, height=6, color=GRAY, fill_opacity=0.2)
            label = Text("img-11.jpeg", font_size=24).move_to(image)
            image = Group(image, label)
        
        self.add(image)

        # 7x7 grid (NumberPlane)
        grid = NumberPlane(
            x_range=[-3.5, 3.5, 1],
            y_range=[-3.5, 3.5, 1],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=6,
            background_line_style={
                "stroke_color": BLUE,
                "stroke_width": 2,
                "stroke_opacity": 0.5
            },
            axis_config={"display_numbers": False}
        )
        self.add(grid)

        # 2. Highlight Specific Cell (center of item)
        cell_unit = grid.x_length / 7
        target_cell_pos = grid.c2p(1, 1) # Cell at (1,1)
        
        selected_cell = Rectangle(
            width=cell_unit,
            height=cell_unit,
            color=YELLOW,
            stroke_width=5,
            fill_opacity=0.3
        ).move_to(target_cell_pos)
        
        # 3. Define Boxes (Ground Truth and Predicted)
        # Ground Truth - using DashedVMobject (standard in Manim CE)
        gt_rect = Rectangle(
            width=cell_unit * 2.5,
            height=cell_unit * 1.5,
            color=RED,
            stroke_width=4
        ).move_to(grid.c2p(1.3, 1.2))
        ground_truth = DashedVMobject(gt_rect)

        # Predicted Box
        predicted_box = Rectangle(
            width=cell_unit * 1.8,
            height=cell_unit * 2.2,
            color=BLUE,
            stroke_width=4
        ).move_to(grid.c2p(0.7, 0.8))

        # 4. Animation Sequence
        self.play(Create(selected_cell))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Create(ground_truth), Create(predicted_box))
        
        # 5. Arrows for (x, y) and (w, h) differences
        p_center = predicted_box.get_center()
        g_center = ground_truth.get_center()
        
        # X and Y distance arrows
        x_arrow = Arrow(start=[p_center[0], p_center[1], 0], end=[g_center[0], p_center[1], 0], 
                        color=GREEN, buff=0, stroke_width=3, max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.2)
        y_arrow = Arrow(start=[g_center[0], p_center[1], 0], end=[g_center[0], g_center[1], 0], 
                        color=ORANGE, buff=0, stroke_width=3, max_tip_length_to_length_ratio=0.2)
        
        # Width/Height difference indicator (visualized as expansion arrows)
        w_arrow = DoubleArrow(
            start=predicted_box.get_right(),
            end=predicted_box.get_right() + RIGHT * (ground_truth.width - predicted_box.width)/2,
            color=PINK, buff=0, stroke_width=3
        )

        self.play(GrowFromCenter(x_arrow), GrowFromCenter(y_arrow))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 6. Formula Presentation
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\lambda_{coord}\sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}\mathbb{1}_{ij}^{obj}\left[(x_{i}-\hat{x}_{i})^{2}+(y_{i}-\hat{y}_{i})^{2}\right]",
            r"+ \sum_{i=0}^{S^{2}}\sum_{j=0}^{B}\mathbb{1}_{ij}^{obj}\left[(\sqrt{w_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{w}_{i}})^{2}+(\sqrt{h_{i}}-\sqrt{\hat{h}_{i}})^{2}\right]",
            font_size=30
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.3).set_background_stroke(color=BLACK, width=5)
        
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Mock Training: Align Boxes
        # Highlight (x,y) part of formula
        self.play(formula[0].animate.set_color(YELLOW))
        self.play(
            predicted_box.animate.move_to(g_center),
            FadeOut(x_arrow),
            FadeOut(y_arrow),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        
        # Highlight (w,h) part of formula
        self.play(
            formula[0].animate.set_color(WHITE),
            formula[1].animate.set_color(YELLOW),
            GrowArrow(w_arrow)
        )
        
        self.play(
            predicted_box.animate.stretch_to_fit_width(gt_rect.width).stretch_to_fit_height(gt_rect.height),
            FadeOut(w_arrow),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        
        self.play(formula[1].animate.set_color(WHITE))
        self.wait(2)

```json
{
  "id": "concept-04-selective-layer-activation",
  "voice": "The breakthrough in efficiency came from 'targeted' fine-tuning. Instead of retraining the whole YOLO model, the team found that the early layers\u2014the backbone\u2014already knew how to see basic shapes. By freezing most of the model and only 'activating' the high-level semantic layers, specifically layers 7 through 10 and the final classification head at layer 23, they achieved 86 percent accuracy. It\u2019s like keeping a person\u2019s general vision intact but specifically training their brain to recognize the nuances of 20 types of garbage.",
  "goal": "Visualize the strategy of freezing specific model layers to optimize training performance.",
  "remarks": "Create a horizontal chain of 24 'Square' objects representing layers 0 to 23. Color layers 0-6 gray (labeled 'Frozen Backbone'). Color layers 7-10 bright blue with a 'Glow' effect (labeled 'Active Semantics'). Color layers 11-22 gray. Color layer 23 bright orange (labeled 'Active Head'). Animate a 'Succession' of pulses moving through the blue and orange squares while the gray ones remain static, representing gradients updating only in selected regions.",
  "formula": null,
  "image_ref": "img-10.jpeg"
}
```

Use the 'voice' for narration, 'goal' for the objective, and 'remarks' for the detailed animation blueprint.
generated_code:
from manim import *

class SelectiveLayerActivation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create a horizontal chain of 24 'Square' objects representing layers 0 to 23
        layers = VGroup(*[Square(side_length=0.3) for _ in range(24)])
        layers.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.1)
        layers.move_to(ORIGIN)

        # Color segments based on the logic provided
        # 0-6: Frozen Backbone (Gray)
        for i in range(0, 7):
            layers[i].set_color(GRAY)
        
        # 7-10: Active Semantics (Blue)
        for i in range(7, 11):
            layers[i].set_color(BLUE)
            
        # 11-22: Frozen intermediate layers (Gray)
        for i in range(11, 23):
            layers[i].set_color(GRAY)
            
        # 23: Active Head (Orange)
        layers[23].set_color(ORANGE)

        # Labels
        frozen_label = Text("Frozen Backbone", font_size=16, color=GRAY).next_to(layers[0:7], UP, buff=0.4)
        semantics_label = Text("Active Semantics", font_size=16, color=BLUE).next_to(layers[7:11], DOWN, buff=0.4)
        head_label = Text("Active Head", font_size=16, color=ORANGE).next_to(layers[23], UP, buff=0.4)

        # Visual Glow Effect for active layers (7-10 and 23)
        active_indices = [7, 8, 9, 10, 23]
        glows = VGroup()
        for idx in active_indices:
            # Create a slightly larger, semi-transparent copy to simulate a glow
            glow = layers[idx].copy()
            glow.set_stroke(layers[idx].get_color(), width=10, opacity=0.3)
            glow.scale(1.2)
            glows.add(glow)

        # Add initial static elements
        self.add(layers, frozen_label, semantics_label, head_label, glows)

        # Animate pulses moving through the blue squares (7-10) and the orange square (23)
        # Using a list of Indicate animations
        pulses = [
            Indicate(layers[i], color=layers[i].get_color(), scale_factor=1.5)
            for i in active_indices
        ]

        # Execute the succession of pulses
        self.play(
            Succession(*pulses),
            run_time=3
        )

        self.wait(2)

When one of these zones struggles to stay cool, it sends a cooling request to the central Air Handler. In response, the handler drops the supply air temperature for everyone. This effectively overcools the majority of the building just to satisfy the high demand of a few special rooms.",
```json
  "goal": "Understand the physical relationship between individual zone requests and building-wide HVAC behavior.",
  "remarks": "Create a central rectangle representing an 'AHU' and five smaller rectangles surrounding it as 'Zones'. Animate a heat gradient (orange) growing in two zones while others remain green. Show dashed arrows moving from the orange zones to the AHU labeled 'Cooling Request'. As arrows hit the AHU, change its color from light blue to deep blue. Then, show deep blue 'Supply Air' arrows flowing back to ALL zones. The green zones should then turn a 'chilled' white-blue color to signify overcooling.",
  "formula": null,
  "image_ref": "img-12.jpeg"
}
```

from manim import *

class DominantZoneMechanism(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Explicit hex color definitions to ensure compatibility across Manim versions
        # BLUE_C equivalent
        C_NORMAL_BLUE = "#58C4DD"
        # BLUE_E equivalent
        C_DEEP_BLUE = "#236B8E"
        # BLUE_A equivalent
        C_LIGHT_BLUE = "#C7E9F1"
        
        # 1. Setup the Central AHU and surrounding Zones
        # AHU is at the top
        ahu = Rectangle(color=C_NORMAL_BLUE, width=2.5, height=1.2, fill_opacity=0.8)
        ahu_label = Text("AHU", font_size=24).move_to(ahu)
        ahu_group = VGroup(ahu, ahu_label).to_edge(UP, buff=1)

        # Five smaller rectangles for Zones at the bottom
        # Each zone is a VGroup of a Rectangle and its label
        zones = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(
                Rectangle(color=GREEN, width=1.5, height=1, fill_opacity=0.4),
                Text(f"Zone {i+1}", font_size=16).shift(DOWN * 0.7)
            ) for i in range(5)
        ])
        zones.arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.5).to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.5)

        self.add(ahu_group, zones)

        # 2. Animate heat gradient (orange) growing in two zones (indices 1 and 3)
        dominant_indices = [1, 3]
        heat_animations = [
            zones[i][0].animate.set_color(ORANGE).set_fill(ORANGE, opacity=0.8)
            for i in dominant_indices
        ]
        
        self.play(*heat_animations, run_time=2)

        # 3. Show dashed arrows moving from the orange zones to the AHU labeled 'Cooling Request'
        request_arrows = VGroup(*[
            DashedLine(
                zones[i].get_top(), 
                ahu.get_bottom(), 
                color=ORANGE, 
                dash_length=0.1
            ).add_tip()
            for i in dominant_indices
        ])
        request_label = Text("Cooling Request", font_size=20, color=ORANGE).next_to(request_arrows, RIGHT, buff=0.5)

        self.play(Create(request_arrows), Write(request_label), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 4. As arrows hit AHU, change color from normal blue to deep blue
        self.play(
            ahu.animate.set_color(C_DEEP_BLUE).set_fill(C_DEEP_BLUE, opacity=1),
            FadeOut(request_label),
            run_time=1
        )

        # 5. Show deep blue 'Supply Air' arrows flowing back to ALL zones
        supply_arrows = VGroup(*[
            Arrow(
                ahu.get_bottom(), 
                zones[i].get_top(), 
                color=C_DEEP_BLUE, 
                buff=0.1, 
                stroke_width=4
            )
            for i in range(5)
        ])
        supply_label = Text("Supply Air", font_size=20, color=C_DEEP_BLUE).next_to(supply_arrows, LEFT, buff=0.5)

        self.play(Create(supply_arrows), Write(supply_label), run_time=2)

        # 6. The non-dominant green zones should then turn a 'chilled' white-blue color to signify overcooling
        overcool_animations = [
            zones[i][0].animate.set_color(C_LIGHT_BLUE).set_fill(C_LIGHT_BLUE, opacity=0.4)
            for i in range(5) if i not in dominant_indices
        ]
        
        self.play(*overcool_animations, run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(2)

Use TransformMatchingTex to show how the feature vector X scales the weights in the logistic formula to move the probability P(y=1) between 0 and 1.",
```json
  "formula": "P(y=1) = \\frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\\beta_0 + \\beta_1 X_{1d} + \\beta_{30} X_{30d})}}",
```

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class LogisticRegressionFeatures(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create Axes for the sigmoid curve
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-6, 6, 2],
            y_range=[0, 1.2, 0.5],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=4,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        
        # Sigmoid curve function
        def sigmoid(x):
            return 1 / (1 + np.exp(-x))

        sigmoid_curve = axes.plot(sigmoid, color=BLUE)
        curve_label = MathTex(r"P(y=1)", font_size=24, color=BLUE).next_to(axes, UP)
        
        self.play(Create(axes), Create(sigmoid_curve), Write(curve_label))

        # Value trackers for the two features
        val_1d = ValueTracker(0)
        val_30d = ValueTracker(0)

        # UI for features (Sliding Bars)
        bar_height = 3
        bar_width = 0.5
        
        bg_1d = Rectangle(width=bar_width, height=bar_height, stroke_color=WHITE).shift(LEFT * 4.5)
        bg_30d = Rectangle(width=bar_width, height=bar_height, stroke_color=WHITE).shift(LEFT * 3.2)
        
        label_1d = Text("1-Day T-CSP", font_size=16).next_to(bg_1d, DOWN)
        label_30d = Text("30-Day T-CSP", font_size=16).next_to(bg_30d, DOWN)

        # Bar fills that respond to value trackers
        # Mapping tracker range [-3, 3] to bar height [0, bar_height]
        fill_1d = always_redraw(lambda: Rectangle(
            width=bar_width,
            height=np.clip(np.interp(val_1d.get_value(), [-3, 3], [0, bar_height]), 0.01, bar_height),
            fill_color=YELLOW,
            fill_opacity=0.7,
            stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(bg_1d.get_bottom(), aligned_edge=DOWN))

        fill_30d = always_redraw(lambda: Rectangle(
            width=bar_width,
            height=np.clip(np.interp(val_30d.get_value(), [-3, 3], [0, bar_height]), 0.01, bar_height),
            fill_color=ORANGE,
            fill_opacity=0.7,
            stroke_width=0
        ).move_to(bg_30d.get_bottom(), aligned_edge=DOWN))

        self.play(
            Create(bg_1d), Create(bg_30d),
            Write(label_1d), Write(label_30d)
        )
        self.add(fill_1d, fill_30d)

        # Dot on the curve representing current prediction
        # Formula: z = X_1d + X_30d (assuming weights = 1 for simplicity)
        dot = always_redraw(lambda: Dot(
            point=axes.c2p(val_1d.get_value() + val_30d.get_value(), sigmoid(val_1d.get_value() + val_30d.get_value())),
            color=GREEN
        ))
        
        self.play(Create(dot))

        # Animate sliding bars and moving dot
        self.play(val_1d.animate.set_value(2), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(val_30d.animate.set_value(2), run_time=1.5)
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(val_1d.animate.set_value(-2.5), val_30d.animate.set_value(-1), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # Mathematical Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            "P(y=1) = \\frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\\beta_0 + \\beta_1 X_{1d} + \\beta_{30} X_{30d})}}",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.3)
        
        # Simplified vector version
        formula_vector = MathTex(
            "P(y=1) = \\frac{1}{1 + e^{-\\theta^T X}}",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.3)

        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Transform the formula to show the abstraction
        # Using ReplacementTransform as a robust alternative to TransformMatchingTex
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(formula, formula_vector))
        self.wait(1)

        # Closing animation: slide bars to show mapping
        self.play(val_1d.animate.set_value(1.0), val_30d.animate.set_value(1.5), run_time=2)
        
        final_msg = Text("Memory features scale weights for prediction.", font_size=24).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Write(final_msg))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class Concept03TheElbowThreshold(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Axes
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=5,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True},
            x_axis_config={"numbers_to_include": [0, 0.5, 1]},
            y_axis_config={"numbers_to_include": [0, 0.5, 1]},
        ).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
        
        labels = axes.get_axis_labels(
            x_label=Text("Fraction of Zones Flagged", font_size=18),
            y_label=Text("Recall", font_size=18)
        )

        # 2. Define the Curve (Concave, rising sharply then leveling off)
        # Using 1 - exp(-k*x) normalized to [0,1]
        def curve_func(x_val):
            k = 5
            return (1 - np.exp(-k * x_val)) / (1 - np.exp(-k))
        
        curve = axes.plot(curve_func, x_range=[0, 1], color=BLUE)
        
        # 3. Floorplan Visualization (Side Panel)
        # Create a grid of squares representing building zones
        floorplan = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.4, stroke_width=2, color=WHITE) 
            for _ in range(20)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=4, cols=5, buff=0.1)
        floorplan.to_corner(UP + RIGHT, buff=1)
        
        floorplan_label = Text("Zone Status", font_size=24).next_to(floorplan, UP)
        
        # 4. Interactive Elements (ValueTracker and Dot)
        tracker = ValueTracker(0.1)
        
        dot = Dot(color=YELLOW)
        # Fixed the lambda variable to 'd' to avoid potential identifier issues
        dot.add_updater(lambda d: d.move_to(axes.c2p(tracker.get_value(), curve_func(tracker.get_value()))))
        
        # Updater for floorplan squares lighting up based on fraction flagged
        def update_squares(group):
            val = tracker.get_value()
            num_to_light = int(val * 20)
            for i, sq in enumerate(group):
                if i < num_to_light:
                    sq.set_fill(GOLD, opacity=0.8)
                else:
                    sq.set_fill(BLACK, opacity=0)
                    
        floorplan.add_updater(update_squares)

        # 5. Narration Text (Side Panel)
        narration_text = Text(
            "We have to decide on a \nprobability threshold.",
            font_size=22,
            line_spacing=1.2
        ).next_to(floorplan, DOWN, buff=1)

        # 6. The Animation Sequence
        self.add(axes, labels, curve, dot, floorplan, floorplan_label, narration_text)
        
        # Move tracker to the right (Flagging many zones)
        # Replaced 'linear' identifier with a lambda to ensure compatibility
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(0.9), run_time=3, rate_func=lambda t: t)
        
        mid_text = Text(
            "Too low: many zones flagged.\nToo high: we miss requests.",
            font_size=22,
            line_spacing=1.2,
            t2c={"low": RED, "high": RED}
        ).move_to(narration_text)
        
        self.play(Transform(narration_text, mid_text))
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(0.05), run_time=2, rate_func=lambda t: t)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Move to the Elbow point
        elbow_val = 0.35
        final_text = Text(
            "The 'elbow' is the sweet spot.",
            font_size=22,
            color=GOLD
        ).move_to(narration_text)
        
        self.play(Transform(narration_text, final_text))
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(elbow_val), run_time=1.5)
        
        # Highlight the Elbow with a glowing circle
        elbow_coord = axes.c2p(elbow_val, curve_func(elbow_val))
        elbow_circle = Circle(radius=0.4, color=GOLD).move_to(elbow_coord)
        elbow_circle.set_stroke(width=6)
        
        self.play(Create(elbow_circle))
        self.play(Flash(elbow_circle, color=GOLD, flash_radius=0.5))
        
        # Show formula reference
        formula = MathTex(r"\text{Recall} = \frac{TP}{TP + FN}", font_size=30)
        formula.to_corner(DL, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        
        self.wait(3)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class Concept03TheElbowThreshold(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Axes
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=5,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
        
        x_label = axes.get_x_axis_label("Fraction\\ of\\ Zones\\ Flagged").scale(0.5)
        y_label = axes.get_y_axis_label("Recall").scale(0.5)
        
        # 2. Mathematical function for the "Elbow" curve (concave)
        def elbow_function(val):
            # Using val^0.25 to create a curve that rises sharply then levels off
            return val**0.25

        curve = axes.plot(elbow_function, x_range=[0.01, 1.0], color=BLUE)
        
        # 3. ValueTracker to control the "Fraction of Zones Flagged"
        tracker = ValueTracker(0.05)
        
        # 4. Moving Point on the curve
        moving_dot = Dot(color=YELLOW)
        moving_dot.add_updater(
            lambda mobj: mobj.move_to(
                axes.c2p(tracker.get_value(), elbow_function(tracker.get_value()))
            )
        )
        
        # 5. Building Floorplan (Side Panel)
        floorplan_title = Text("Building Floorplan", font_size=24).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1.0).shift(UP * 3)
        
        # Create a grid of 25 zones
        zones = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.4, fill_opacity=0.1, stroke_width=1, color=WHITE)
            for _ in range(25)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=5, cols=5, buff=0.1).next_to(floorplan_title, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        
        # Updater to light up squares based on the fraction tracker
        def update_zones(group):
            fraction = tracker.get_value()
            # How many of the 25 zones should be 'flagged' (yellow)
            num_flagged = int(fraction * 25)
            for idx, zone in enumerate(group):
                if idx < num_flagged:
                    zone.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=0.8)
                    zone.set_stroke(YELLOW, width=2)
                else:
                    zone.set_fill(BLACK, opacity=0.1)
                    zone.set_stroke(WHITE, width=1)
        
        zones.add_updater(update_zones)
        
        # 6. Formula and Title
        formula = MathTex(r"Recall = \frac{TP}{TP + FN}").scale(0.8).to_corner(UL)
        
        # --- ANIMATION ---
        
        # Initial display
        self.play(
            Create(axes),
            Write(x_label),
            Write(y_label),
            Write(formula),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.play(Create(curve), run_time=2)
        self.play(
            FadeIn(floorplan_title),
            Create(zones),
            FadeIn(moving_dot),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Move right: flagging more zones (high recall, but low selectivity)
        # Using linear rate for steady movement
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(0.9), run_time=4, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Move left: flagging fewer zones (high selectivity, but missing recall)
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(0.02), run_time=3, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Move to the "Elbow" point
        elbow_fraction = 0.15
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(elbow_fraction), run_time=2)
        
        # Highlight the elbow with a glowing effect
        elbow_coord = axes.c2p(elbow_fraction, elbow_function(elbow_fraction))
        glow_circles = VGroup(*[
            Circle(radius=r, color=YELLOW, stroke_opacity=1 - (r/0.4), stroke_width=2)
            for r in [0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4]
        ]).move_to(elbow_coord)
        
        elbow_text = Text("The 'Elbow'", color=YELLOW, font_size=24).next_to(glow_circles, UR, buff=0.2)
        
        self.play(
            FadeIn(glow_circles),
            Write(elbow_text),
            Flash(elbow_coord, color=YELLOW)
        )
        
        self.wait(3)
        
        # Cleanup
        self.play(
            FadeOut(VGroup(axes, x_label, y_label, curve, moving_dot, formula, zones, floorplan_title, glow_circles, elbow_text)),
            run_time=1.5
        )

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class TheElbowThreshold(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Axes and Labels
        ax = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=5,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5)
        
        labels = ax.get_axis_labels(
            x_label="Fraction\\ of\\ Zones", 
            y_label="Recall"
        )
        labels[0].scale(0.6).shift(DOWN * 0.3)
        labels[1].scale(0.6).shift(LEFT * 0.3)

        # 2. Formula
        formula = MathTex(r"Recall = \frac{TP}{TP + FN}").scale(0.7).to_corner(UL)

        # 3. Curve (Concave, steep then levels off)
        # Using f(x) = (1 - e^(-5x)) / (1 - e^-5) to normalize [0,1]
        def elbow_func(x):
            return (1 - np.exp(-5 * x)) / (1 - np.exp(-5))
        
        plot = ax.plot(elbow_func, x_range=[0, 1], color=BLUE)
        
        # 4. ValueTracker and Moving Point
        tracker = ValueTracker(0.1)
        
        dot = Dot(color=YELLOW)
        dot.add_updater(lambda mobj: mobj.move_to(ax.c2p(tracker.get_value(), elbow_func(tracker.get_value()))))
        
        # 5. Side Panel: Floorplan
        # Creating a 5x5 grid of squares to represent zones
        floorplan_title = Text("Building Floorplan", font_size=24).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1.5).shift(UP * 2.5)
        zones = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.4, stroke_width=2, color=WHITE) 
            for _ in range(25)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=5, cols=5, buff=0.1).next_to(floorplan_title, DOWN, buff=0.5)

        def update_zones(group):
            # The number of zones highlighted corresponds to the tracker value (fraction of zones)
            num_to_highlight = int(tracker.get_value() * 25)
            for i, zone in enumerate(group):
                if i < num_to_highlight:
                    zone.set_fill(RED, opacity=0.8)
                else:
                    zone.set_fill(BLACK, opacity=0)

        zones.add_updater(update_zones)

        # 6. Elbow Highlight (Static circle at the "sweet spot")
        elbow_point_x = 0.35 # Approx elbow for -5x decay
        elbow_point = ax.c2p(elbow_point_x, elbow_func(elbow_point_x))
        elbow_circle = Circle(radius=0.3, color=YELLOW).move_to(elbow_point)
        elbow_glow = elbow_circle.copy().set_stroke(width=10, opacity=0.3)
        elbow_label = Text("The 'Elbow'", font_size=20, color=YELLOW).next_to(elbow_circle, UR, buff=0.1)
        elbow_group = VGroup(elbow_circle, elbow_glow, elbow_label)

        # 7. Animation sequence
        self.add(ax, labels, formula, floorplan_title, zones)
        self.play(Create(plot), run_time=2)
        self.add(dot)
        
        # Rate function is linear by default in some contexts, but we specify it for clarity
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(0.9), run_time=4, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(0.1), run_time=3, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(elbow_point_x), run_time=2)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(elbow_group))
        self.play(Indicate(elbow_group))
        
        # Final note
        note = Text("Optimizing Catch Rate vs. Efficiency", font_size=24, color=GREEN).next_to(zones, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(note))
        
        self.wait(3)

import numpy as np
from manim import *

class AdaptiveVSStaticSelection(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Background and Split Screen Setup
        self.camera.background_color = BLACK
        
        static_title = Text("Static Benchmark (ASHRAE)", font_size=28, color=BLUE).to_edge(UP).shift(LEFT * 3.5)
        adaptive_title = Text("Logistic Model (ML)", font_size=28, color=GREEN).to_edge(UP).shift(RIGHT * 3.5)
        divider = Line(UP * 4, DOWN * 1, color=WHITE)
        self.add(static_title, adaptive_title, divider)

        # 2. Floorplan Creation
        def create_floorplan(pos):
            floor = VGroup(*[Square(side_length=0.7, fill_opacity=0.2, color=GREY) for _ in range(9)])
            floor.arrange_in_grid(rows=3, cols=3, buff=0.15)
            floor.move_to(pos)
            return floor

        static_floor = create_floorplan(LEFT * 3.5 + UP * 1)
        adaptive_floor = create_floorplan(RIGHT * 3.5 + UP * 1)
        
        # Static side: Set specific zones to be permanently "flagged" (red)
        static_indices = [0, 4, 8]
        for i in static_indices:
            static_floor[i].set_fill(RED, opacity=0.8)
            static_floor[i].set_color(RED)
            
        self.add(static_floor, adaptive_floor)

        # 3. Timeline and Navigation
        timeline_line = Line(LEFT * 5, RIGHT * 5, color=GREY).shift(DOWN * 1.5)
        months = ["May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep"]
        month_labels = VGroup(*[Text(m, font_size=24) for m in months])
        month_labels.arrange(RIGHT, buff=1.5).next_to(timeline_line, DOWN)
        cursor = Dot(color=YELLOW).move_to(timeline_line.get_start())
        self.add(timeline_line, month_labels, cursor)

        # 4. Graphing (Performance Comparison)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 10, 1],
            y_range=[0, 100, 20],
            x_length=10,
            y_length=1.5,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.3)
        
        graph_label = Text("Missed Requests (Recall Error)", font_size=20).next_to(axes, UP, buff=0.1)
        
        # Tracking variable for synchronization
        time_tracker = ValueTracker(0)

        # Static Line: Consistently high error
        static_error_line = axes.plot(lambda x: 75, color=BLUE)
        static_tag = Text("Static", color=BLUE, font_size=18).next_to(static_error_line, RIGHT, buff=0.1)

        # ML Line: Dynamic and lower error
        # Note: 'x' is defined locally within the lambda passed to axes.plot
        ml_error_line = always_redraw(lambda: axes.plot(
            lambda x_val: 20 + 25 * np.exp(-((x_val - 5)**2) / 2), 
            x_range=[0, max(0.01, time_tracker.get_value())],
            color=GREEN
        ))
        
        self.add(axes, graph_label, static_error_line, static_tag, ml_error_line)

        # 5. Adaptive Logic (Updaters)
        def update_adaptive_floor(mob):
            t = time_tracker.get_value()
            # Heat intensity peaks in mid-summer (t=5)
            heat_intensity = np.exp(-((t - 5)**2) / 4)
            
            # Logic: More zones turn red as heat increases
            for i, square in enumerate(mob):
                # Use a pseudo-random but time-dependent threshold for flickering effect
                threshold = 0.2 + 0.6 * heat_intensity
                noise = (np.sin(i * 3 + t * 2) + 1) / 2
                if noise < threshold:
                    square.set_fill(RED, opacity=0.8)
                    square.set_color(RED)
                else:
                    square.set_fill(GREY, opacity=0.2)
                    square.set_color(GREY)

        adaptive_floor.add_updater(update_adaptive_floor)

        # 6. Animation Execution
        # Ensure 'linear' is accessed from the rate_functions module
        self.play(
            time_tracker.animate.set_value(10),
            cursor.animate.move_to(timeline_line.get_end()),
            run_time=12,
            rate_func=rate_functions.linear
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class AdaptiveVSStaticSelection(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Main Titles and Dividers
        title = Text("Adaptive ML vs. Static ASHRAE Selection", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        labels = VGroup(
            Text("Static Benchmark", color=BLUE, font_size=24).shift(3.5 * LEFT + 2.5 * UP),
            Text("Logistic Model (ML)", color=GREEN, font_size=24).shift(3.5 * RIGHT + 2.5 * UP)
        )
        divider = Line(UP * 2.2, DOWN * 1.5, color=WHITE)
        self.add(title, labels, divider)

        # 2. Timeline at the bottom
        # x_range: May(5) to Sept(9). Using Axes instead of NumberLine to ensure compatibility.
        timeline = Axes(
            x_range=[5, 9, 1],
            y_range=[0, 1, 1],
            x_length=10,
            y_length=0,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).shift(DOWN * 3)
        timeline.get_y_axis().set_opacity(0)
        
        month_names = ["May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep"]
        month_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(month_names[i], font_size=18).next_to(timeline.c2p(5+i, 0), DOWN)
            for i in range(5)
        ])
        
        time_tracker = ValueTracker(5)
        pointer = Triangle(color=YELLOW, fill_opacity=1).scale(0.15).rotate(PI)
        pointer.add_updater(lambda m: m.move_to(timeline.c2p(time_tracker.get_value(), 0) + UP * 0.2))
        
        self.add(timeline, month_labels, pointer)

        # 3. Floorplan visualization
        def get_floorplan(offset):
            frame = Rectangle(width=4.5, height=3, color=WHITE, stroke_width=2, fill_opacity=0.05).move_to(offset)
            # Create a grid of "zones" (dots)
            dots = VGroup()
            for x in np.linspace(-1.8, 1.8, 5):
                for y in np.linspace(-1, 1, 4):
                    dots.add(Dot(point=frame.get_center() + [x, y, 0], radius=0.1, color=GRAY))
            return VGroup(frame, dots)

        left_fp = get_floorplan(3.5 * LEFT + 0.5 * UP)
        right_fp = get_floorplan(3.5 * RIGHT + 0.5 * UP)
        
        # Static side: some zones stay highlighted RED forever
        for i in [2, 7, 12, 18]:
            left_fp[1][i].set_color(RED)
        
        self.add(left_fp, right_fp)

        # 4. Adaptive Animation Logic
        # The right floorplan zones flicker and change count based on time_tracker
        def adaptive_update(m):
            t = time_tracker.get_value()
            # Calculate "stress" level (higher in middle of summer, peak at t=7)
            stress = np.exp(-0.5 * (t - 7)**2) * 10 
            for i, dot in enumerate(m):
                # Deterministic pseudo-randomness based on index and time
                if (i * 1.3 + t * 5) % 10 < stress:
                    dot.set_color(GREEN)
                else:
                    dot.set_color(GRAY)

        right_fp[1].add_updater(adaptive_update)

        # 5. Missed Requests Graph
        graph_axes = Axes(
            x_range=[5, 9, 1],
            y_range=[0, 100, 20],
            x_length=6,
            y_length=1.5,
            axis_config={"font_size": 14}
        ).shift(DOWN * 1.2)
        
        graph_title = Text("Missed Requests (%)", font_size=14).next_to(graph_axes, UP, buff=0.1)
        
        # Static: Higher error, ML: Consistently lower error
        static_plot = graph_axes.plot(lambda x: 60 + 10 * np.sin(x), color=RED)
        ml_plot = graph_axes.plot(lambda x: 15 + 5 * np.cos(x*3), color=GREEN)
        
        graph_group = VGroup(graph_axes, graph_title, static_plot, ml_plot)
        self.add(graph_group)

        # 6. Description Text
        narrative = Text(
            "ML stays adaptive, reducing missed requests during summer peaks.",
            font_size=18, color=YELLOW
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.1)

        # 7. Execution
        self.add(narrative)
        # Using lambda for rate_func to avoid potential undefined identifier 'linear'
        self.play(
            time_tracker.animate.set_value(9), 
            run_time=10, 
            rate_func=lambda t: t
        )
        self.wait(2)

```json
{
  "id": "concept-01-contrastive-alignment",
  "voice": "To figure out where a photo was taken, we first need to align visual features with geographic concepts. We use a CLIP model to create a shared mathematical space for images and text. During training, we feed the model a street view image alongside a synthetic caption like 'A Street View photo in France.' The goal is to maximize the cosine similarity between the image embedding and its correct country label while pushing it away from all others. This contrastive approach teaches the model that architectural styles, vegetation, and road signs are all linked to specific country names.",
  "goal": "Understand how contrastive learning aligns images with country-specific text descriptions in a shared embedding space.",
  "remarks": "Create two neural network blocks labeled 'Image Encoder' and 'Text Encoder'. Show a Street View image entering the Image Encoder and the text 'A Street View photo in France' entering the Text Encoder. Represent the output as two vectors (arrows) in a 2D coordinate system. Animate the two vectors rotating to point in the same direction. Then, show a 'wrong' text label like 'A Street View photo in Japan' and show its vector being pushed away from the image vector. Use a ValueTracker to show the similarity score increasing as they align.",
  "formula": "\\mathcal{L}_{CLIP} = 0.5 \\cdot (\\mathcal{L}_{Text} + \\mathcal{L}_{Images})",
  "image_ref": "img-7.jpeg"
}
```

Use the 'voice' for narration, 'goal' for the objective, and 'remarks' for the detailed animation blueprint.

\mathcal{L}_{CLIP} = 0.5 \cdot (\mathcal{L}_{Text} + \mathcal{L}_{Images})

from manim import *

class ContrastiveAlignment(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Encoders and UI
        img_enc_rect = Rectangle(height=1.2, width=2.5, color=BLUE)
        img_enc_text = Text("Image Encoder", font_size=20).move_to(img_enc_rect.get_center())
        img_enc = VGroup(img_enc_rect, img_enc_text)

        txt_enc_rect = Rectangle(height=1.2, width=2.5, color=GREEN)
        txt_enc_text = Text("Text Encoder", font_size=20).move_to(txt_enc_rect.get_center())
        txt_enc = VGroup(txt_enc_rect, txt_enc_text)
        
        encoders = VGroup(img_enc, txt_enc).arrange(DOWN, buff=1.5).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1)
        
        input_img = Square(side_length=1, fill_opacity=0.8, fill_color=BLUE).next_to(img_enc, LEFT, buff=1.5)
        input_img_label = Text("Street View", font_size=16).next_to(input_img, DOWN)
        
        input_txt_fr = Text("'...in France'", font_size=18).next_to(txt_enc, LEFT, buff=1.5)
        
        # 2. Embedding Space
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-1.5, 1.5, 1], 
            y_range=[-1.5, 1.5, 1], 
            x_length=4, 
            y_length=4, 
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1)
        space_label = Text("Shared Embedding Space", font_size=22).next_to(axes, UP)
        origin_point = axes.coords_to_point(0, 0)
        
        # 3. Vectors and Labels
        v_img = Arrow(origin_point, axes.coords_to_point(1, 0.5), buff=0, color=YELLOW)
        v_txt = Arrow(origin_point, axes.coords_to_point(-0.5, 1), buff=0, color=GREEN)
        
        v_img_lab = Text("Image", color=YELLOW, font_size=18)
        v_img_lab.add_updater(lambda mob: mob.next_to(v_img.get_end(), UR, buff=0.1))
        
        v_txt_lab = Text("Text (France)", color=GREEN, font_size=18)
        v_txt_lab.add_updater(lambda mob: mob.next_to(v_txt.get_end(), UL, buff=0.1))

        # 4. Similarity Score and Formula
        sim_tracker = ValueTracker(0.12)
        sim_label = Text("Cosine Similarity:", font_size=24).to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.2).shift(LEFT * 1)
        sim_val = DecimalNumber(sim_tracker.get_value(), num_decimal_places=2, color=WHITE).next_to(sim_label, RIGHT)
        sim_val.add_updater(lambda mob: mob.set_value(sim_tracker.get_value()))

        formula = MathTex(r"\mathcal{L}_{CLIP} = 0.5 \cdot (\mathcal{L}_{Text} + \mathcal{L}_{Images})", font_size=28).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.4)

        # --- ANIMATION ---
        self.add(encoders, space_label, axes, formula)
        self.play(FadeIn(input_img), FadeIn(input_img_label), FadeIn(input_txt_fr))
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Process Inputs
        self.play(
            input_img.animate.move_to(img_enc.get_center()).scale(0.2).set_opacity(0),
            input_txt_fr.animate.move_to(txt_enc.get_center()).scale(0.2).set_opacity(0),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        
        # Show Vectors
        self.play(GrowArrow(v_img), GrowArrow(v_txt), FadeIn(v_img_lab), FadeIn(v_txt_lab))
        self.play(FadeIn(sim_label), FadeIn(sim_val))
        self.wait(1)

        # Align Vectors (Positive Pairing)
        target_v_img = Arrow(origin_point, axes.coords_to_point(1, 1), buff=0, color=YELLOW)
        target_v_txt = Arrow(origin_point, axes.coords_to_point(1, 1), buff=0, color=GREEN)
        
        self.play(
            Transform(v_img, target_v_img),
            Transform(v_txt, target_v_txt),
            sim_tracker.animate.set_value(1.00),
            run_time=2.5
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Contrastive Step (Negative Pairing)
        input_txt_jp = Text("'...in Japan'", font_size=18).move_to(txt_enc.get_left()).shift(LEFT * 1.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(input_txt_jp))
        
        self.play(
            input_txt_jp.animate.move_to(txt_enc.get_center()).scale(0.2).set_opacity(0),
            run_time=1
        )
        
        # Switch text vector label and push away
        v_txt_lab_new = Text("Text (Japan)", color=RED, font_size=18)
        # We manually manage the label transformation to avoid conflicts with updaters
        v_txt_lab.clear_updaters()
        v_txt_lab_new.add_updater(lambda mob: mob.next_to(v_txt.get_end(), DL, buff=0.1))
        
        pushed_v_txt = Arrow(origin_point, axes.coords_to_point(-1, -0.5), buff=0, color=RED)
        
        self.play(
            v_txt.animate.set_color(RED),
            Transform(v_txt_lab, v_txt_lab_new),
            Transform(v_txt, pushed_v_txt),
            sim_tracker.animate.set_value(-0.45),
            run_time=2.5
        )
        self.wait(2)
        
        # Final cleanup
        v_img_lab.clear_updaters()
        v_txt_lab_new.clear_updaters()
        sim_val.clear_updaters()
        self.play(FadeOut(VGroup(v_img, v_txt, v_img_lab, v_txt_lab, sim_label, sim_val)))
        self.wait(1)
